name: nmse_gf16_bf16
description: Golden vector set for the GF16 vs bfloat16 NMSE comparison protocol described in GF16_BFLOAT16_NMSE.md. These vectors pin the seeds, input distributions, reference baseline, and tolerance bands a candidate harness MUST honour. No row claims a measured silicon NMSE; the `nmse_*_db_expected` fields are SPEC tolerance bands, not observations.

r5_honesty.note: All numbers below are SPEC bounds. A measured run lands under docs/silicon/ and cites this vector set by name + sha256 to be considered valid under the protocol.

protocol.delta_db_definition: Delta_dB = NMSE_GF16_dB - NMSE_bf16_dB; positive => GF16 worse; negative => GF16 better
protocol.reference_algorithm: bit-exact same reduction as candidate; no fused MAC tricks

delta_db_expected.note: Δ_dB ≤ +0 required to publish 'GF16 competitive at WL1'

workloads.label: Adversarial / CLARA refusal path (Demo 2)
workloads.reason: Output surface is K3 (TRUE/UNKNOWN/FALSE), not a numeric vector. Demo 2 uses pass/fail rules, not NMSE.

workloads.label: Proof-trace / audit-receipt mesh route (Demo 3)
workloads.reason: Output is a hash; exact match required. NMSE does not apply.

invariants: GF16 field widths in `specs/numeric/gf16.t27` must match (sign=1, exp=6, mant=9, bias=31).
BF16 field widths in `conformance/FORMAT-SPEC-001.json` must match (sign=1, exp=8, mant=7, bias=127).
Any run with samples < samples_min is INVALID under the protocol.
Any run without `rtl_hash` and `t27_release_tag` pinned is INVALID.
